title: What Goes On Tour

jokes.text: Wouldn't have to be that big to be our biggest gig ever.
jokes.setup: Murray announces their biggest gig ever, suggesting it's significant
jokes.punchline: Bret deadpans that any gig would qualify as their biggest
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-deprecating humor that establishes the band's lack of success with economy and character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Wouldn't have to be that big to be our biggest gig ever.

jokes.text: Headlining at Central Park? Yes. Us? Yes. Me and Bret? Yes! Bret as well?
jokes.setup: Murray confirms they're headlining Central Park
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's incredulous questioning escalates to asking if Bret will also be there
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating disbelief that builds to absurd specificity about his own bandmate

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Headlining at Central Park? Yes. Us? Yes. Me and Bret? Yes!

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Bret as well?

jokes.text: I've dipped into the Flight of the Conchords emergency fund.
jokes.setup: Murray explains how he afforded the tour
jokes.punchline: The reveal of an 'emergency fund' for this unsuccessful band
jokes.explanation: Sets up the mystery of where Murray got money, with mild humor in the idea of an emergency fund for this band

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I've dipped into the Flight of the Conchords emergency fund.

jokes.text: Players play the game, you guys play the guitar. Don't they? They kick the ball, you kick the... jams.
jokes.setup: Murray compares managing a rugby tour to managing a band
jokes.punchline: The awkward attempt to make 'kick the jams' parallel to 'kick the ball'
jokes.explanation: Murray's tortured logic and the awkward coining of 'kick the jams' is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Players play the game, you guys play the guitar. Don't they? They kick the ball, you kick the... jams.

jokes.text: So we have to get up at 7:00 in the morning and do 200 squat-thrusts?
jokes.setup: Murray gives them rugby training itinerary
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's literal interpretation of rugby training for musicians
jokes.explanation: Solidly sets up Murray's incompetence at adapting rugby plans to music

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: So we have to get up at 7:00 in the morning and do 200 squat-thrusts?

jokes.text: Yeah, you crossed out 'bus,' and you wrote 'bus.'
jokes.setup: Murray tries to explain his itinerary modifications
jokes.punchline: Bret points out Murray crossed out 'bus' and wrote 'bus' back
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Murray's bureaucratic incompetence rendered absurd

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Yeah, you crossed out 'bus,' and you wrote 'bus.'

jokes.text: 'Honda Accord' was too big to fit in the gap there, so I put 'bus' back in.
jokes.setup: Murray explains his confusing itinerary edits
jokes.punchline: His solution was based purely on what would fit in the space
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Murray's bureaucratic mindset where form matters more than function

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: 'Honda Accord' was too big to fit in the gap there, so I put 'bus' back in.

jokes.text: Sometimes late at night when I'm driving alone, I imagine this is a bus.
jokes.setup: Murray wants to share a secret with Bret
jokes.punchline: His pathetic fantasy about his Honda being a tour bus
jokes.explanation: Beautifully pathetic character moment that reveals Murray's delusions and loneliness

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Sometimes late at night when I'm driving alone, I imagine this is a bus.

jokes.text: Is that... a bit silly, is it, Bret?
jokes.setup: Murray shares his bus fantasy
jokes.punchline: His vulnerable need for validation about his silly fantasy
jokes.explanation: Extends the cringe of Murray's vulnerability and need for approval

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Is that... a bit silly, is it, Bret?

jokes.text: You asked me why I was crying. I said it was the happiest day of my life. You don't remember that?
jokes.setup: Murray mentions being back together with Shelly
jokes.punchline: The absurd detail about crying from happiness that the band didn't notice
jokes.explanation: Shows how oblivious the band is to Murray's personal life despite his oversharing

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You asked me why I was crying. I said it was the happiest day of my life. You don't remember that?

jokes.text: those two effing idiots are good friends of mine, okay?
jokes.setup: Murray on phone with angry wife about spending their money
jokes.punchline: Calling his clients 'effing idiots' while defending them
jokes.explanation: Murray's conflicted relationship with the band - insulting them while defending them

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: those two effing idiots are good friends of mine, okay?

jokes.text: I'm not even managing them anymore.
jokes.setup: Murray trying to appease his angry wife
jokes.punchline: Claiming he's not managing them while literally on tour managing them
jokes.explanation: Simple irony of Murray lying while actively managing them

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I'm not even managing them anymore.

jokes.text: It's probably Mel stalking us.
jokes.setup: Murray's paranoia about being followed
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's casual recognition that it's just their stalker fan
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and callback, showing how normalized Mel's stalking has become

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: It's probably Mel stalking us.

jokes.text: What a coincidence! Ha ha!
jokes.setup: Mel appears after being mentioned as their stalker
jokes.punchline: Her obviously fake surprise at 'coincidentally' running into them
jokes.explanation: Confirms the stalking callback with Mel's transparent 'coincidence' act

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: What a coincidence! Ha ha!

jokes.text: That's where the gig is, Jemaine. It's at the airport.
jokes.setup: Jemaine confused why they're at an airport
jokes.punchline: The 'gig' is at the airport hotel lounge, not a real venue
jokes.explanation: Reveals Murray's continued incompetence at booking appropriate venues

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: That's where the gig is, Jemaine. It's at the airport.

jokes.text: Shut up, Bret. Don't do your embarrassing speeches.
jokes.setup: Bret starts thanking the audience
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's blunt interruption calling his speeches embarrassing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamics - Jemaine's bluntness vs Bret's earnestness

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Shut up, Bret. Don't do your embarrassing speeches.

jokes.text: Well, it's only the middle of the afternoon, though.
jokes.setup: Murray calls it a great night
jokes.punchline: Bret's literal correction that it's afternoon
jokes.explanation: Bret's typical literal-minded response that deflates Murray's enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Well, it's only the middle of the afternoon, though.

jokes.text: There's hardly anyone here.
jokes.setup: Murray praising the crowd
jokes.punchline: Bret's blunt observation about the poor attendance
jokes.explanation: Another example of Bret puncturing Murray's delusions with simple truth

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: There's hardly anyone here.

jokes.text: A lot of people have gone to get their flights
jokes.setup: Bret points out poor attendance
jokes.punchline: Murray's weak excuse that people left for flights
jokes.explanation: Murray's defensive rationalization about why their gig failed

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: A lot of people have gone to get their flights

jokes.text: Not leather suits. It's a total waste of money.
jokes.setup: Murray explaining per diems are for necessities
jokes.punchline: Reveals the band bought leather suits with their meal money
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag about the leather suits and their poor financial decisions

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Not leather suits. It's a total waste of money.

jokes.text: You've spent tomorrow's per diem. The per diem I gave you for was for the week.
jokes.setup: Bret asks for advance on tomorrow's per diem
jokes.punchline: Murray reveals they've already spent a week's worth of money
jokes.explanation: Escalates the financial irresponsibility and sets up the Latin joke

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You've spent tomorrow's per diem. The per diem I gave you for was for the week.

jokes.text: 'Per diem' means for the day, though. See? Well, I don't know Latin. Well, you should have said...'per weekem,' would be the correct term.
jokes.setup: Jemaine corrects Murray's use of 'per diem'
jokes.punchline: Murray's fake Latin 'per weekem' as if that's a real term
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake Latin wordplay that shows Murray's pompous ignorance perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: 'Per diem' means for the day, though. See?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Well, I don't know Latin.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Well, you should have said...'per weekem,' would be the correct term.

jokes.text: You're like a couple of cool-looking idiots.
jokes.setup: Murray angry at their spending
jokes.punchline: The backhanded compliment that they look cool but are idiots
jokes.explanation: Perfect oxymoron that captures Murray's frustration with their style vs substance

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You're like a couple of cool-looking idiots.

jokes.text: Do not call me at work, all right? I don't want them asking silly questions as to why I'm not at work
jokes.setup: Murray on phone giving instructions
jokes.punchline: The circular logic of not wanting work to know he's not at work
jokes.explanation: Murray's flawed logic about hiding his absence reveals his poor planning

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Do not call me at work, all right? I don't want them asking silly questions as to why I'm not at work

jokes.text: Okay, love you too! Bye-bye!
jokes.setup: Murray having tense phone conversation with wife
jokes.punchline: His sudden cheerful 'love you too' after hanging up
jokes.explanation: Shows Murray trying to maintain normalcy after a clearly difficult conversation

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Okay, love you too! Bye-bye!

jokes.text: You must have followed him, did you? What, don't you think I'm capable of a classic rock 'n' roll move?
jokes.setup: Murray assumes Jemaine led the leather suit purchase
jokes.punchline: Bret's mild offense at being seen as uncreative
jokes.explanation: Shows Murray's assumptions about their personalities and Bret's rare assertion of independence

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You must have followed him, did you?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: What, don't you think I'm capable of a classic rock 'n' roll move?

jokes.text: We're on tour now. Brings out the crazier side of someone like him.
jokes.setup: Murray warning Bret about Jemaine on tour
jokes.punchline: The idea that mild-mannered Jemaine has a 'crazy side'
jokes.explanation: Murray's overblown concerns about Jemaine's nonexistent wild side

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: We're on tour now. Brings out the crazier side of someone like him.

jokes.text: Probably looking for some thunderstorms to rock out in.
jokes.setup: Murray finds out Jemaine was watching weather
jokes.punchline: Murray's wild misinterpretation that he wants to rock in storms
jokes.explanation: Murray's absurd rock star fantasies projected onto mundane weather watching

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Probably looking for some thunderstorms to rock out in.

jokes.text: Gotta be in exactly the right place. It's your turn to hold it, man. My arms are sore.
jokes.setup: They're trying to watch TV in their hotel room
jokes.punchline: They have to manually hold the TV antenna in exact position
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy about their poor accommodations and primitive TV reception

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Gotta be in exactly the right place. It's your turn to hold it, man. My arms are sore.

jokes.text: $8 can of mixed nuts. They must have been pretty good. Got so much buzz out of them that you chucked a television out the window.
jokes.setup: Murray sees expensive minibar charge
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic theory that nuts gave such a buzz they caused TV-throwing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd causality linking expensive nuts to rock star destruction

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: $8 can of mixed nuts. They must have been pretty good. Got so much buzz out of them that you chucked a television out the window.

jokes.text: I know how rock 'n' roll he thinks he is... Mr. Keith Moon.
jokes.setup: Murray thinking Jemaine threw the TV
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically comparing mild Jemaine to wild drummer Keith Moon
jokes.explanation: Perfect rock reference that highlights the absurdity of comparing Jemaine to a legendary wild man

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I know how rock 'n' roll he thinks he is... Mr. Keith Moon.

jokes.text: You're watching Jemaine with his rock 'n' roll moves, buying the leather suits, getting the mixed nuts, jumping on the bed. And you thought, 'Oh, how can I equal this?'
jokes.setup: Murray explaining Bret's motivation for throwing TV
jokes.punchline: Lists mundane activities as 'rock 'n' roll moves' culminating in bed-jumping
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating list that treats buying nuts and jumping on beds as epic rock rebellion

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You're watching Jemaine with his rock 'n' roll moves, buying the leather suits, getting the mixed nuts, jumping on the bed. And you thought, 'Oh, how can I equal this?'

jokes.text: No, I picked them out.
jokes.setup: Murray asks if there are cashews in the mixed nuts
jokes.punchline: Bret's matter-of-fact admission he picked out the best nuts
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Bret's selfishness delivered with complete innocence

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: No, I picked them out.

jokes.text: These are the guys that destroyed all that equipment the other night in Passaic!
jokes.setup: Murray introduces the band at new venue
jokes.punchline: They're already notorious for destruction at previous venues
jokes.explanation: Escalates their reputation for destruction beyond just the TV incident

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: These are the guys that destroyed all that equipment the other night in Passaic!

jokes.text: Becky thought you guys were probably just a couple of magicians, and Kathy, Lisa, Tawny and Tracy thought you guys were probably just a couple of gays.
jokes.setup: Water polo player explains their bet about the band
jokes.punchline: The leather suits suggested either magicians or gay men
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about how leather suits are interpreted by others

dialogue.line: Becky thought you guys were probably just a couple of magicians, and Kathy, Lisa, Tawny and Tracy thought you guys were probably just a couple of gays.

jokes.text: Are you sexy nurses?
jokes.setup: Learning they're all dressed the same and asking if they're a band
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's random guess they might be sexy nurses
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random Jemaine logic and wishful thinking delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Are you sexy nurses?

jokes.text: Do we? Pfft. Do we? Yeah, it's... everyone plays it. It's our national sport.
jokes.setup: Asked if New Zealand has water polo
jokes.punchline: Their ridiculous escalating claims about water polo being huge in NZ
jokes.explanation: Great escalating lies to impress the girls, perfectly in character for their desperation

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Do we? Pfft. Do we?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's... everyone plays it. It's our national sport.

jokes.text: Bret Bret? No, just joking. It's McKenzie.
jokes.setup: Girl mishears his name and asks for his last name
jokes.punchline: Bret's attempt at humor by pretending his name is 'Bret Bret'
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that shows Bret's awkward attempts at humor under pressure

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Bret Bret? No, just joking. It's McKenzie.

jokes.text: Don't joke, Bret, it's confusing when you joke.
jokes.setup: Bret makes weak joke about his name
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's blunt assessment that Bret shouldn't attempt humor
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamics - Jemaine's brutal honesty about Bret's comedic abilities

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Don't joke, Bret, it's confusing when you joke.

jokes.text: Throw another shrimp on the barbie. Crikey! Wha!
jokes.setup: Asked to say Australian phrase despite being from New Zealand
jokes.punchline: Bret gives up and just does the accent anyway
jokes.explanation: Shows Bret's people-pleasing nature overriding his principles about NZ vs Australia

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Throw another shrimp on the barbie. Crikey! Wha!

jokes.text: On sea horses?
jokes.setup: Hearing water polo is like polo but in water
jokes.punchline: Bret's literal interpretation that they'd ride sea horses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical absurdity - perfectly reasonable yet completely wrong conclusion delivered innocently

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: On sea horses?

jokes.text: Mermaids song sequence
jokes.setup: Comparing water polo players to mermaids
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fantasy song about mermaid relationships and logistics
jokes.explanation: Classic FOTC musical comedy - absurd romantic fantasy with specific weird details about mermaid anatomy and underwater logistics

dialogue.line: Mermaids song sequence

jokes.text: Or are you an optical illusion caused by a woman sitting on a rock holding half a fish?
jokes.setup: Romantic song about mermaids
jokes.punchline: Sudden deflating rational explanation for mermaid sightings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of romantic fantasy with absurdly specific rational explanation

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Or are you an optical illusion caused by a woman sitting on a rock holding half a fish?

jokes.text: we've been waiting for five hours
jokes.setup: Waiting for the water polo team to show up
jokes.punchline: The pathetic length of time they've been waiting
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe humor about their pathetic romantic situation

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: we've been waiting for five hours

jokes.text: Look, just pour some tap water and blow some bubbles in it
jokes.setup: Band asking for sparkling water from minibar
jokes.punchline: Murray's ridiculous DIY sparkling water solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray solution - technically logical but completely missing the point

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Look, just pour some tap water and blow some bubbles in it

jokes.text: Oh no, that's my autograph.
jokes.setup: Murray asks if signature on bill is Bret's
jokes.punchline: Bret's proud clarification that it's an autograph, not just a signature
jokes.explanation: Shows Bret's naive pride and delusions about his celebrity status

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Oh no, that's my autograph.

jokes.text: This is your doing, isn't it, Rod Stewart?
jokes.setup: Murray angry about the expensive bar tab
jokes.punchline: Calling Bret 'Rod Stewart' as if he's a wild rock star
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd comparison of mild Bret to famously wild rock star Rod Stewart

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: This is your doing, isn't it, Rod Stewart?

jokes.text: Oh, they're in the car.
jokes.setup: Asked for car keys to move the car
jokes.punchline: Keys are locked inside the car that needs to be moved
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for the car-in-pool disaster, not particularly original but functional

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Oh, they're in the car.

jokes.text: Well that's what happened. The Amer... on American cars, the hand brake's on this side. Oh, I must have just adjusted the seat.
jokes.setup: Car somehow ended up in the pool
jokes.punchline: Weak excuse about American car differences and seat adjustment
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of their tour disasters with weak rationalization

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Well that's what happened. The Amer... on American cars, the hand brake's on this side. Oh, I must have just adjusted the seat.

jokes.text: First Jemaine with the mixed nuts, then the TV. And finally, Bret, you drive my Honda into the pool!
jokes.setup: Murray listing their tour disasters
jokes.punchline: The escalating sequence from nuts to TV to car in pool
jokes.explanation: Good escalating callback that builds the pattern of their destructive tour

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: First Jemaine with the mixed nuts, then the TV. And finally, Bret, you drive my Honda into the pool!

jokes.text: Personally, I prefer the mixed nuts caper.
jokes.setup: Murray listing their disasters
jokes.punchline: His casual preference for the 'mixed nuts caper' as if it's a heist
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray line - treating nuts as a sophisticated crime worthy of preference

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Personally, I prefer the mixed nuts caper.

jokes.text: Well, we all... we've all made a mistake each, so it sort of evens out.
jokes.setup: Murray angry about multiple disasters
jokes.punchline: Bret's innocent logic that equal mistakes cancel each other out
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bret logic - mathematically sound but completely missing the point of escalating damage

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Well, we all... we've all made a mistake each, so it sort of evens out.

jokes.text: I'm so angry I feel like swearing. Oh Murray, you wouldn't swear at us. Go fuck yourself, Bret!
jokes.setup: Murray threatening to swear, Bret saying he wouldn't
jokes.punchline: Murray immediately proves him wrong with shocking profanity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character break - polite Murray's sudden profanity is perfectly shocking and quotable

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I'm so angry I feel like swearing.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Oh Murray, you wouldn't swear at us.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Go fuck yourself, Bret!

jokes.text: But get it wet and it snugs up, it'll shrink... shrink to fit. Yeah, I can't even get mine off.
jokes.setup: Giving Murray a leather suit as apology
jokes.punchline: The suits are comically oversized and Jemaine is stuck in his
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy callback to the leather suits with additional absurdity of poor fit

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: But get it wet and it snugs up, it'll shrink... shrink to fit.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yeah, I can't even get mine off.

jokes.text: There there there there there there.
jokes.setup: Murray comforting himself about being a bad manager
jokes.punchline: The excessive repetition of 'there there' as self-comfort
jokes.explanation: Murray's pathetic self-comfort taken to absurd repetitive extremes

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: There there there there there there.

jokes.text: Yes it is. It's a central park. But it is a central park.
jokes.setup: Discovering they're not at Central Park, New York
jokes.punchline: Murray's semantic defense that it IS 'a' central park
jokes.explanation: Murray's desperate wordplay to defend his misleading promise about Central Park. Technical truth vs. actual deception.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yes it is. It's a central park. But it is a central park.

jokes.text: Yes it is. It's a central park. But it is a central park. I said a central park in Newark.
jokes.setup: Band realizes this isn't Central Park New York
jokes.punchline: Murray's semantic argument about 'central park' vs 'Central Park'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay reveal that explains Murray's deception through semantic trickery

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yes it is. It's a central park. But it is a central park. I said a central park in Newark.

jokes.text: I think I've broken a piece of them.
jokes.setup: Murray making excuse to his boss about missing work
jokes.punchline: His medically nonsensical claim about breaking 'a piece' of his leg muscles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray medical nonsense delivered with bureaucratic confidence

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I think I've broken a piece of them.

tmdb_overview: Using the band's "emergency fund," Murray organizes a warm-up tour in preparation for a big gig in Central Park. At an airport bar, the boys get hooked by some "mermaids" from a woman's water-polo team - nearly ruining the tour and their relationship with Murray.
